description_long: This cowshed is a long, rectangular agricultural building with a pitched gable roof covered in reddish-brown tiles. The structure is primarily constructed from light-colored wood, with open bays supported by wooden posts along one side, providing sheltered but accessible space for livestock. The main entrance features a large sliding barn door, and there are additional open sections that allow for easy movement of animals and ventilation. The roof extends well beyond the walls, creating overhangs that offer protection from the elements. The building appears to have minimal windows, focusing instead on open wall sections for airflow. The overall design is utilitarian, emphasizing function and durability for housing cattle in a rural or peri-urban setting.
description: A long, wooden cowshed with a reddish-brown gable roof, open bays, and a large sliding barn door. The structure is functional and airy, designed for livestock shelter.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main sliding barn door, open bays supported by wooden posts, and the full length of the gable roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the narrow profile of the building, the overhanging roof, and two open entry bays.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a large open section and the continuation of the gable roof, with fewer visible entry points.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the narrow end of the building, roof overhang, and a single supporting post.
attribute_door_style: sliding barn door, wooden construction with horizontal planks
attribute_window_presence: no windows, open wall sections for ventilation
